title: Login with Email Containing Uppercase Letters Authenticates Successfully (Case-Insensitive Email Matching)
description: Verifies that the authentication system treats email addresses as case-insensitive per RFC 5321, correctly normalizing 'PETRA.Vondrak@CloudBase.EU' to match the stored 'petra.vondrak@cloudbase.eu' account. This is a real-world production bug: case-sensitive email comparison causes login failures for users whose email clients auto-capitalize, and it intermittently breaks 'remember me' or SSO flows.
expected_result: PASS: Login succeeds with mixed-case email, user lands on /dashboard, and profile displays the canonical lowercase email. The system correctly normalizes email identifiers. FAIL: System returns 'User not found' or 'Invalid credentials' error despite correct password (case-sensitive comparison bug), or the profile displays the mixed-case input as the stored email (normalization not applied at storage time).

test_steps.action: Fill in the email using mixed-case — different casing from what is stored in the database
test_steps.expected: Email field contains the mixed-case input

test_steps.action: Fill in the correct password for this account

test_steps.expected: User is authenticated and redirected to /dashboard — case difference in email was normalized

test_steps.action: Wait for the authenticated dashboard to render
Verify the displayed email in the user profile/account settings matches the canonical lowercase version, not the uppercased input
test_steps.expected: Profile page loads showing the user's stored email address

test_steps.action: Confirm the email shown in the profile is the normalized lowercase form
test_steps.expected: Profile email field shows 'petra.vondrak@cloudbase.eu' (lowercase canonical form, not the uppercased input)
